data.title: Jensen Huang Says It 'Might Be the Last Time' Nvidia (NVDA) Invests in OpenAI
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said that the chipmaker's recent $30 billion investment in OpenAI may be its final investment in the artificial intelligence startup before it potentially goes public later this year. Speaking at the Morgan Stanley (MS) Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Huang explained that Nvidia had previously discussed the possibility of investing as much as $100 billion in OpenAI as part of an infrastructure partnership. However, he noted that such a large investment is now unlikely, mostly because OpenAI is expected to pursue an IPO in the near future.

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 03/04/2026
data.description:
Investors seeking reliable income can look to long-established dividend aristocrats to supply a substantial share of their portfolio cash flows with consistent distributions through 2026. These include Hormel Foods (HRL), Kimberly-Clark (KMB), and Stanley Black & Decker (SWK), identified using the TipRanks Dividend Aristocrat tool.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock currently has a dividend payout ratio of at least 100%, meaning that each company recently distributed at least their entire net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. Hormel Foods (HRL) — This is an American packaged foods giant that develops, processes, and distributes various meat, nuts, and food products to domestic and international retail, foodservice, deli, and commercial customers. The Minnesota-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 170.95%. HRL paid 29 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.68%. Its shares currently have a Moderate Buy consensus rating from the two top Wall Street analysts covering the stock, with an average price target of $28, which implies about 15% upside.
  2. Kimberly-Clark (KMB) — This American corporation makes personal care and consumer tissue products such as disposable diapers, paper towels, and napkins that are sold across the world. The Texas-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 133.86%. It paid $1.28 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.59%. KMB stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 10 Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $124.89, implying about 20% upside.
  3. Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) — This U.S. manufacturer makes tools and accessories for both domestic and commercial usage. This includes items such as corded and cordless electric power tools, hydraulic equipment, and industrial coating equipment. The Connecticut-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 141.33%. The firm paid 83 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 3.93%. SWK stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 11 Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $89.90, which implies about 12% upside.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Dividend aristocrats within the S&P 500 index (SPX) are a small group of companies distinguished by increasing their dividends every year for at least 25 straight years, not just holding payouts flat. This characteristic tends to appeal to investors who prioritize steadily growing income streams rather than reacting to short-term share price swings. 

Newly created spin-off firms can sometimes join this club if they are viewed as having effectively continued their former parent company's long-running record of consistent dividend growth.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

To find more stocks like these, take a look at TipRanks' Dividend Aristocrats tool.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 3/4/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are rallying on Wednesday as investors digest two positive economic data points.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ADP reported that private employers added 63,000 jobs in February, the most since July 2025 and above the consensus estimate of 50,000. However, most of the growth was attributed to the education/health services and construction sectors, which added a combined 77,000 jobs. No other sector delivered more than 11,000 jobs, while the professional/business services sector lost 30,000 jobs.

ISM Services PMI Jumps to 3-Year High

Meanwhile, the ISM Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 56.1 in February, marking the highest level since July 2022 and above the estimate of 53.8. The index measures the health of the U.S. services sector by tracking new orders, employment, business activity, and supplier deliveries. A reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 points to contraction.

Every component of the index expanded for the first time since March 2021, while prices paid fell to 63.0, the lowest reading since March 2025 and signaling easing inflation.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Oil Could Skyrocket to $100, Warns Goldman Sachs
data.description:
The price for a barrel of Brent crude oil (BZ) has risen by 10% over the past week, although Goldman Sachs warns that extended disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz could send prices even higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The strait handles 20% of global oil flows and has been operating at roughly 15% capacity since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran over the weekend. Goldman's worst-case scenario is for the passage to continue operating at a low volume for five weeks, which could send prices above $100. Another factor that may increase prices is damage to oil infrastructure.

Oil Prices Could Spike Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions

Goldman's base case is for the strait to operate at 15% capacity for five more days, followed by a month-long recovery to normal volumes. Earlier this week, the firm raised its second-quarter Brent crude oil price target by $10 to $76. The firm also lifted its fourth-quarter estimate to $66.

Earlier this week, JPMorgan warned that oil prices could surge to $120 in the event of a prolonged U.S.-Iran conflict, adding that Gulf producers could only maintain normal production for 25 days in the event that the Strait of Hormuz is completely blocked.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: COIN, MSTR, HOOD: Crypto Stocks Surge on Bitcoin Strength and Regulatory Tailwinds
data.description:
Cryptocurrency stocks Coinbase (COIN), Strategy (MSTR), and Robinhood (HOOD) have surged over 15%, 12%, and 8%, respectively, on Wednesday, primarily due to a massive Bitcoin rally, renewed institutional interest, and positive regulatory developments. 

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bitcoin's Rally Lifts Crypto‑Linked Stocks 

BTC approached $73,000 in today's session, marking its highest point in nearly a month. This "V-shaped recovery" from weekend lows around $63,000 has been fueled by institutional demand and massive Spot Bitcoin ETF inflows.

COIN directly benefits from increased trading volume and revenue as crypto prices rise. MSTR functions as a leveraged proxy for Bitcoin because it is the largest corporate holder, currently owning 713,502 BTC. 

Bitcoin miners, Marathon Digital (MARA), CleanSpark (CLSK), and Riot Platforms (RIOT) saw notable gains today.

Trump Support for Crypto

President Trump signaled support for the cryptocurrency industry today, specifically siding with crypto firms in an ongoing regulatory battle with traditional banks. He posted on Truth Social, demanding that legacy banks stop "undermining" his administration's crypto agenda.

Trump specifically backed the ability of crypto firms to offer interest-like rewards on stablecoins, arguing that "Americans should earn more money on their money."

Trump urged Congress to immediately pass the CLARITY Act, a bill that would establish a definitive regulatory framework for digital assets. This helped boost sentiment across crypto‑linked stocks.       

Institutional Buying Aided Rally

Cathie Wood's ARK Invest added more than $16 million in Coinbase and Robinhood shares, taking advantage of lower prices as geopolitical tensions and broader market weakness pressured tech and crypto stocks.

ARK's decision to increase exposure suggests it views the pullback as temporary and believes platforms tied to crypto trading and adoption remain well-positioned for future growth.

Which Stock Is a Better Buy?

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all six companies witnessing a surge today and see which stock analysts favor. According to analysts, Strategy stock has the highest upside potential of 110.93%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Tesla Model Y Completes Huge FSD Run Without Intervention, Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Surges
data.description:
Finally, a day in which the news about a company matches what happens in its stock price. Those who were concerned about just how well electric vehicle giant Tesla's (TSLA) Full-Self Driving (FSD) product works will have reason to reconsider. A Tesla Model Y recently made a run from Raleigh, North Carolina to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania with absolutely no intervention. That news buoyed investors' spirits and sent shares up over 3% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While Tesla's FSD system remains FSD (supervised), a Level 2 system that still requires human oversight, it managed to do a fine job of proving that the supervision part is more decorative than anything. The Model Y in question managed to handle a drive of 415 miles without incident, and without the human driver having to intervene at any point.

Moreover, the Tesla in question ran into a variety of driving conditions and seemed to perform without issue. The driver noted, "When I left, it was 58F and raining. When I arrived home, it was 35F, with snow on the ground. FSD handled heavy rain, light rain, day, night, declining temps, heavy traffic, construction, etc. All flawlessly."

Waiting for Optimus

Meanwhile, one of Tesla's other big draws, the Optimus robot, is expected to get a bit of a show this month. The latest version, Gen 3, was expected to be shown off in the first quarter of 2026. A simple look at a calendar shows that that quarter has about three and a half weeks left to it, so an event should be in the works here.

There are already some looking for a bit of a design change to hit. Adam Jonas, Morgan Stanley analyst with a four-star rating on TipRanks, noted, "Recent comments from Elon Musk would lead us to believe the Gen 3 update will specifically emphasize dexterity…and manufacturability…don't be surprised if Optimus is simpler than you'd expect."

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.61% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $399.25 per share implies 1.12% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Strategy Stock Has Price Target Slashed – But Is Still a Buy, Says Clear Street
data.description:
Bitcoin seems to be staging a recovery at the moment, up by 7% over the past day, so it's no surprise to find Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) shares are also on the move, up by 11% at the time of writing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The gain in Wednesday's session should boost sentiment among investors, as the stock has endured a brutal pullback, shedding 59% over the past 6 months.

The depressed price action mirrors the general downbeat trajectory Bitcoin has taken and has led Clear Street analyst Brian Dobson to revise his outlook. Reflecting "persistently lower BTC prices and more conservative BTC yield assumptions," Dobson has lowered his target from $268 to $198, although the revised figure still makes room for 12-month appreciation of 34%. Dobson's rating stays a Buy. (To watch Dobson's track record, click here)

While Dobson is less optimistic on the near-term action, he keeps an overall bullish stance following a recent meeting with Strategy's executive chairman and co-founder Michael Saylor and president and CEO Phong Le. "We continue to view MicroStrategy as a highly differentiated and scalable vehicle for Bitcoin exposure," said Dobson. "The company's treasury strategy has proven durable, with BTC holdings now exceeding 673k BTC. While volatility remains elevated, we believe disciplined balance sheet management, a growing NAV base, and consistent growth in Bitcoin per share remain core drivers of long-term value creation."

As for key takeaways from the meeting, Dobson says the company plans to lean into preferred share issuance in the first half of 2026, using "structured capital" to fund additional BTC purchases while preserving "equity optionality" and strengthening STRC's "institutional positioning." STRC refers to Strategy's perpetual preferred stock that pays variable dividends, has no maturity, and was created to raise capital efficiently. Management emphasized that STRC is the company's strategic priority and a cornerstone of its next phase in capital markets development. The goal isn't just to hold BTC, but to build an institutional-grade instrument that offers deep, liquid trading, lower volatility than spot BTC, and operates as a fully regulated crypto credit vehicle.

On the regulatory front, management sees the next major milestone coming after passage of the Clarity Act: a potential US withdrawal from the Basel framework, which currently imposes steep capital charges on BTC holdings, effectively discouraging meaningful balance sheet exposure. "While likely more than a year away," Dobson said, "reform could materially improve BTC adoption among legacy financial institutions."

The company's thesis is that BTC is evolving from a speculative asset into a "sovereign-adjacent reserve asset." As institutional and sovereign capital shifts from gold to BTC, its scale grows, volatility drops, and it becomes "balance sheet infrastructure." At that stage, a BTC credit sector can emerge, and MSTR aims to be the "benchmark BTC credit issuer."

"Strategically," Dobson added, "MSTR is evolving from BTC proxy to BTC treasury to BTC-backed credit platform, with STRC serving as the bridge toward sovereign-grade crypto capital market infrastructure."

Turning now to the rest of the Street, where there have been 9 other recent MSTR reviews, all also positive, making the consensus view a Strong Buy. At $310, the average target points toward one-year returns of 111%. (See MSTR stock forecast)

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rebounds on Upbeat Jobs Data amid Global Tariff Update
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Wednesday after ADP released its National Employment Report for February.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the month, private employers added 63,000 jobs, marking the highest growth since July 2025 and topping the consensus estimate of 50,000. However, job growth remains concentrated in certain sectors. The education and health services sector added 58,000 jobs, while the professional and business services sector shed 30,000 jobs.

"But with hiring concentrated in only a few sectors, our data shows no widespread pay benefit from changing jobs," said ADP Chief Economist Dr. Nela Richardson. "In fact, the pay premium for switching employers hit a record low in February."

Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC that President Trump would raise the global tariff rate to 15% from 10% "likely sometime this week." After the Supreme Court declared Trump's tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) illegal, he turned to another legal route, Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974.

The statute allows a maximum tariff rate of 15% for up to 150 days to address a major balance-of-payments deficit. Bessent added that he expects tariff rates to revert to their prior rates before the ruling within five months as the administration conducts Section 301 and 232 investigations.

Before the market open, reports circulated that Iran had reached out to the U.S. to engage in peace talks one day after being attacked. However, Iran denied the claims and characterized them as "pure falsehood and psychological warfare."

The Dow Jones is up by 0.58% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Which Defense ETF Is Most Exposed to the U.S. and Iran Conflict?
data.description:
Rising tensions in the Middle East have pushed defense stocks higher, which has also drawn more attention to defense-focused exchange-traded funds. In particular, three defense ETFs appear to be especially well-positioned to benefit from the escalation involving Iran:

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA)
  • Global X Defense Tech ETF (SHLD)
  • Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA)

However, ITA stands out because it has the largest exposure to major U.S. defense contractors. In fact, the fund is heavily concentrated in companies such as RTX (RTX) and Lockheed Martin (LMT), which produce weapons systems that are in high demand during military operations.

For instance, RTX manufactures Patriot missile systems and Tomahawk missiles, both of which are widely used for regional defense. Meanwhile, Lockheed Martin produces the F-35 fighter jet and THAAD missile interceptors, which are key components of modern military operations.

Different Types of Exposure

At the same time, other defense ETFs provide different types of exposure to the same trend. For example, SHLD focuses more on defense technology and advanced digital systems rather than traditional aerospace hardware. As a result, the fund includes companies like Palantir (PLTR), which analysts believe could benefit from increased demand for AI-powered intelligence, surveillance, and threat detection. Meanwhile, PPA takes a diversified approach by holding 61 companies across the aerospace, defense, and homeland security industries, thereby spreading the risk across the entire defense supply chain.

Wall Street's Take

Turning to Wall Street, of the defense ETFs pictured below, analysts see the most upside potential in the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX). In fact, ARKX's price target of $38.75 per share implies 20.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
